title: How reference databases relate: derivation and coverage
text: Hover node highlight connections; arrows point source database incorporates . Square nodes databases can download dbpq; round nodes upstream (external) sources.
code: library(visNetwork)  dbpq_dbs <- c(   "UNITE", "SILVA", "PR2", "BOLD", "MaarjAM", "Eukaryome",   "Greengenes2", "RDP", "MIDORI2", "Diat.barcode", "KSGP",   "GTDB+", "GTDB_cleaned", "LTPlus" )  all_nodes <- union(c(derivation$from, derivation$to), dbpq_dbs)  nodes <- tibble(id = all_nodes) |>   mutate(     group = if_else(id %in% dbpq_dbs, "dbpq database", "external source"),     label = id   )  edges <- derivation |>   transmute(     from, to,     title = what,     arrows = "to",     color = "#6e2c9b"   )  visNetwork(nodes, edges, height = "560px", width = "100%") |>   visGroups(     groupname = "dbpq database",     shape = "box", color = "#10677c", font = list(color = "white")   ) |>   visGroups(     groupname = "external source",     shape = "dot", color = "#9aa0a6"   ) |>   visEdges(smooth = list(enabled = TRUE, type = "cubicBezier")) |>   visNodes(font = list(size = 18)) |>   visOptions(highlightNearest = list(enabled = TRUE, degree = 1, hover = TRUE)) |>   visHierarchicalLayout(direction = "LR", sortMethod = "directed") |>   visLegend(width = 0.15, position = "right", main = "Node type")

title: How reference databases relate: derivation and coverage
text: information adjacency matrix: filled cell row , column j means “sequences incorporated j”. Reading column tells everything database built ; reading across row tells everywhere database’s sequences end . Annotation-links (taxonomy transferred without sequences) shown lighter shade keep distinct true nestedness.  Three structural facts pop matrix: INSDc (GenBank/EMBL/DDBJ) common root. Almost every database ultimately draws raw sequences INSDc — “independent” means independently curated, never sequence-disjoint source. KSGP sits deepest point. pulls GTDB+, pulls GTDB, PR2 MIDORI2 — three-level chain. Eukaryome, Greengenes2 LTPlus convergence nodes. Several otherwise independent databases flow — Eukaryome merges SILVA, PR2 UNITE, Greengenes2 LTPlus synthesise prokaryote 16S space GTDB SILVA/LTP.
code: lvls <- c(   "INSDc", "GTDB", "Karst 2018", "LTP", "EMP500",   "SILVA", "PR2", "UNITE", "MIDORI2", "EukRef", "EukRibo",   "GTDB_cleaned", "GTDB+",   "Eukaryome", "Greengenes2", "KSGP", "LTPlus",   "MaarjAM", "RDP", "Diat.barcode", "BOLD" )  mat <- bind_rows(   derivation |> transmute(from, to, kind = "sequence incorporation"),   annotation |> transmute(from, to, kind = "annotation only") ) |>   mutate(     from = factor(from, levels = lvls),     to = factor(to, levels = rev(lvls))   )  ggplot(mat, aes(x = from, y = to, fill = kind)) +   geom_tile(color = "white", linewidth = 0.6) +   scale_fill_manual(     values = c(       "sequence incorporation" = "#3e135a",       "annotation only" = "#c9b3da"     ),     name = NULL   ) +   labs(     x = "Source (sequences come FROM)",     y = "Target (... are incorporated INTO)",     title = "Derivation adjacency matrix"   ) +   coord_fixed() +   theme_minimal(base_size = 11) +   theme(     axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),     panel.grid = element_line(color = "grey92"),     legend.position = "top"   )

secondary (contamination-filtering) coverage   "UNITE",         "Viridiplantae",   "ITS",      "secondary",   "UNITE",         "Metazoa",         "ITS",      "secondary",   "PR2",           "Metazoa",         "SSU 18S",  "secondary",   "PR2",           "Fungi",           "SSU 18S",  "secondary",   "PR2",           "Viridiplantae",   "SSU 18S",  "secondary",   "KSGP",          "Protists",        "SSU 18S",  "secondary" )  clade_lvls <- c(   "Bacteria", "Archaea", "Protists", "Fungi", "Glomeromycota",   "Viridiplantae", "Metazoa", "Diatoms" ) db_lvls <- c(   "SILVA", "Greengenes2", "RDP", "KSGP", "LTPlus", "PR2", "UNITE",   "Eukaryome", "BOLD", "MIDORI2", "MaarjAM", "Diat.barcode" )  coverage <- coverage |>   mutate(     clade = factor(clade, levels = rev(clade_lvls)),     database = factor(database, levels = db_lvls)   )  ggplot(coverage, aes(x = database, y = clade)) +   geom_tile(aes(fill = role), color = "white", linewidth = 0.6) +   geom_text(aes(label = marker), size = 2.4, color = "white", lineheight = 0.8) +   scale_fill_manual(     values = c("primary" = "#124444", "secondary" = "#7fa8a8"),     name = "Coverage"   ) +   labs(     x = NULL, y = NULL,     title = "Taxonomic coverage by database (marker annotated)",     subtitle = "Same row = alternatives; different markers = complementary"   ) +   theme_minimal(base_size = 11) +   theme(     axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),     panel.grid = element_blank(),     legend.position = "top"   )

title: How reference databases relate: derivation and coverage
text: Combine two layers designing assignment strategy: Don’t stack nested databases independent evidence. Eukaryome already contains SILVA, PR2 UNITE; running UNITE Eukaryome fungal reads two independent opinions. Likewise KSGP, Greengenes2 LTPlus draw GTDB SILVA/LTP, treat variations one prokaryote 16S resource, independent corroboration. combine genuinely independent, complementary databases. soil eukaryote survey: UNITE (fungal ) + MaarjAM (AMF SSU, clade UNITE -resolves) + PR2 (protist SSU) cover different clades markers independent curation. Pick marker-matched primary database per clade coverage heatmap, use secondary coverage (lighter cells) flag remove -target reads, assign fine rank.

title: Taxonomy formats in reference databases
text: dbpq recognizes five taxonomy formats, grouped two categories: prefix-based formats (rank short prefix like k__) positional formats (ranks identified position semicolon-delimited string).

title: Taxonomy formats in reference databases
text: Used : UNITE (general FASTA releases) rank identified two-letter prefix followed double underscores, separated semicolons:
code: >AB123456;k__Fungi;p__Ascomycota;c__Sordariomycetes;o__Hypocreales;f__Nectriaceae;g__Fusarium;s__oxysporum ATCGATCG...

title: Taxonomy formats in reference databases
text: Used : UNITE (SINTAX release), PR2 (UTAX format), MIDORI2 (SINTAX format), Eukaryome (SINTAX format) Taxonomy encoded tax= prefix, single-letter rank codes followed colons, separated commas: format used vsearch --sintax original USEARCH UTAX algorithm. Note SINTAX UTAX use header format despite different classification algorithms.
code: >AB123456;tax=d:Eukaryota,k:Fungi,p:Ascomycota,c:Sordariomycetes,o:Hypocreales,f:Nectriaceae,g:Fusarium,s:Fusarium_oxysporum ATCGATCG...

title: Taxonomy formats in reference databases
text: Used : Greengenes2 Similar UNITE format starts d__ (domain) instead k__ (kingdom):
code: >abc123 d__Bacteria;p__Pseudomonadota;c__Gammaproteobacteria;o__Enterobacterales;f__Enterobacteriaceae;g__Escherichia;s__Escherichia coli ATCGATCG...

title: Taxonomy formats in reference databases
text: databases use semicolon-separated taxonomy without prefix. meaning rank determined position string. number levels varies database. formats, use rank_position list_ranks_db() extract specific rank position.

title: Taxonomy formats in reference databases
text: Used : SILVA (dada2-formatted), RDP (dada2-formatted) number levels depends specific training set. dada2::assignTaxonomy() classifier accepts number semicolon-separated levels — without prefixes — via taxLevels argument.
code: >Bacteria;Proteobacteria;Gammaproteobacteria;Enterobacterales;Enterobacteriaceae;Escherichia; ATCGATCG...

title: Taxonomy formats in reference databases
text: Used : PR2 (dada2 format) PR2 uses 9 taxonomic levels specific protist taxonomy:
code: >EU293891.1.1750_U Eukaryota;Archaeplastida;Chlorophyta;Chlorophyta_X;Mamiellophyceae;Mamiellales;Bathycoccaceae;Ostreococcus;Ostreococcus_tauri ATCGATCG...

title: Taxonomy formats in reference databases
text: Beyond taxonomy encoding, formats differ sequence name (accession number, e.g. AB123456) appears FASTA header: prefix-based formats (UNITE, SINTAX, Greengenes2), sequence name clearly separated taxonomy string. dada2 positional format used SILVA RDP training sets, typically separate accession number — header contains taxonomy, deepest level often serves sequence identifier. distinction matters converting formats: conversion functions like format2sintax() format2dada2() must correctly extract relocate sequence name.

title: Manage FASTA Reference Databases for Taxonomic Assignment
text: reference database metabarcoding taxonomic assignment characterized seven key properties: Name & source — database name, version, official website (e.g., SILVA, UNITE). Tracking exact version used essential reproducibility. Marker — targeted genetic marker region (e.g., SSU/18S, LSU/28S, ITS1/ITS2, 16S, COI). database generally specific one markers primer pairs. Taxonomic focus — clade(s) database provides sufficient coverage enable reliable taxonomic assignment (e.g., Fungi UNITE, protists PR2, arbuscular mycorrhizal fungi MaarjAM). determines groups can assigned confidence. Secondary coverage — Sequences clades outside primary focus, present low numbers. allow reads non-target organisms classified excluded rather misassigned (e.g., plant animal sequences UNITE). representative sequences per secondary group needed; secondary coverage enable fine-grained assignment within groups. Taxonomic format — taxonomy encoded database: Number files (single annotated FASTA vs. separate sequence + taxonomy files) Use guide tree phylogeny-aware classifiers Handling uninformative ranks (e.g., whether unknown_family unclassified_order allowed headers) Header syntax (SINTAX: tax=k:Fungi,p:Ascomycota; dada2: k__Fungi;p__Ascomycota; custom formats) Clustering — Whether sequences grouped reduce redundancy, : Identity-based clustering (e.g., SILVA 99% sequence identity) Biologically meaningful units (e.g., UNITE Species Hypotheses, SH), collapses intraspecific variation preserving species-level resolution Filtering — Pre-processing steps applied download: Taxonomic scope restriction (e.g., keeping target clade reduce database size) Removal non-informative sequences (e.g., entries assigned unknown_order contribute assignment) Sequence trimming selection (e.g., extracting region ITSx, applying quality scores Pintail values SILVA) dbpq provides tools download pre-built databases, convert header formats, apply custom filtering steps address points 5–7.

text: ¹ “Protists” paraphyletic; PR2 covers SAR, Excavata, Amoebozoa, related unicellular eukaryotic lineages. ² BOLD query-based: covers taxon marker requested. ³ UNITE (eukaryotes version) adds secondary coverage Viridiplantae Metazoa (useful filtering non-fungal reads). ⁴ KSGP includes eukaryote sequences PR2 MIDORI2 contamination control, fine-grained eukaryote assignment.

title: Manage FASTA Reference Databases for Taxonomic Assignment
text: Databases relate two distinct ways: incorporate sequences others (derivation / nestedness), others independently cover clades (parallel use). matrix reads “sequences column database incorporated row database” — every edge verified database’s describing paper. Lighter cells mark annotation-links (taxonomy transferred without sequences). Two relationships easy get wrong: Eukaryome independent — compiles sequences SILVA, PR2 UNITE — Greengenes2 derives GTDB r207 directly (LTP Karst 2018), sharing Karst 2018 source KSGP. full picture — interactive derivation network, derivation matrix, marker × clade coverage heatmap — article reference databases relate. Independent databases — independently curated, cross-database sequence incorporation (though ultimately draw INSDc/GenBank); use parallel based marker taxonomic scope:

title: Manage FASTA Reference Databases for Taxonomic Assignment
code: # 7a — Restrict to a taxonomic scope filter_db("database.fasta", pattern = "Fungi", output = "database_fungi.fasta")  # 7b — Identify non-informative taxa (e.g. "unknown_order", "unclassified") count_unwanted_tax("database.fasta")  # Keep only sequences with family-level annotation (pattern excludes empty f__ fields) filter_db("database.fasta", pattern = "f__[A-Za-z]", output = "database_with_family.fasta")  # 7c — Trim primers and select amplicon-length sequences cutadapt_rm_primers_db(   "database.fasta",   primer_fw = "GCATCGATGAAGAACGCAGC",   primer_rev = "TCCTCCGCTTATTGATATGC",   output = "database_trimmed.fasta" )

what: Annotate query sequences with UNITE Species Hypothesis (SH) names — add_sh_to_taxonomy
title: Annotate query sequences with UNITE Species Hypothesis (SH) names — add_sh_to_taxonomy
text: Runs vsearch --usearch_global match query sequences UNITE reference database, extracts Species Hypothesis (SH) identifier best hit. SH name first |-delimited field UNITE sequence identifiers (e.g. SH123456.09FU). function ports logic nf-core/ampliseq add_sh_to_taxonomy.py script R, using vsearch directly instead requiring external lookup tables.

title: Annotate query sequences with UNITE Species Hypothesis (SH) names — add_sh_to_taxonomy
code: add_sh_to_taxonomy(   query_fasta,   unite_db,   vsearchpath = find_vsearch(),   id = 0.97,   maxaccepts = 1,   maxrejects = 32,   nproc = 1,   top_hits_only = TRUE,   keep_temporary_files = FALSE,   verbose = FALSE )

title: Annotate query sequences with UNITE Species Hypothesis (SH) names — add_sh_to_taxonomy
text: query_fasta (Character) Path FASTA file containing query sequences (e.g. ASVs OTUs). unite_db (Character) Path UNITE reference FASTA file. file can format (SINTAX, UNITE default, etc.) long sequence identifiers contain SH names first |-delimited field. Files downloaded via download_unite_db() meet requirement. vsearchpath (Character) Path vsearch executable. Defaults find_vsearch(). id (Numeric, default 0.97) Minimum sequence identity threshold (0–1) vsearch --id parameter. maxaccepts (Integer, default 1) Maximum number hits accept per query. Set 0 unlimited hits (useful multiple equally-good matches may different SH names). maxrejects (Integer, default 32) Maximum number rejected hits stopping search query. nproc (Integer, default 1) Number threads vsearch. top_hits_only (Logical, default TRUE) TRUE, report top hit per query (highest identity). FALSE, report accepted hits, useful detecting ambiguous SH assignments. keep_temporary_files (Logical, default FALSE) TRUE, delete temporary blast6 output file parsing. verbose (Logical, default FALSE) Print vsearch progress messages.

title: Annotate query sequences with UNITE Species Hypothesis (SH) names — add_sh_to_taxonomy
text: data.frame columns: query: Query sequence identifier. sh_name: Species Hypothesis name extracted best database hit (e.g. SH123456.09FU), NA hit found. target: Full identifier matched database sequence. pct_id: Percent identity match. aln_len: Alignment length. mismatches: Number mismatches. e_value: E-value match. is_ambiguous: Logical; TRUE multiple hits top identity disagree SH name.

Directory save downloaded file. taxon (Character, required) Taxonomic name query (e.g., "Fungi", "Arthropoda", "Mammalia"). marker (Character, default "COI-5P") barcode marker. Common values: "COI-5P", "", "matK", "rbcL". tax_format (Character, default "dada2") Taxonomy format written FASTA headers, file can feed MiscMetabar::add_new_taxonomy_pq(). One : "dada2": unprefixed, positional ranks (>Phylum;Class;Order;Family;Genus;Species;). "sintax": >processid;tax=p:Phylum,c:Class,.... title: Download sequences from BOLD Systems — download_bold_db
text: function uses BOLD public API hosted v3.boldsystems.org, remains available main BOLD site's migration v5. tax_format = "none" queries sequence endpoint (FASTA). "dada2"/"sintax" queries combined endpoint (TSV full ranked taxonomy), keeps requested marker, writes taxonomy-headed FASTA (gaps removed). BOLD's taxonomy starts phylum, dada2 output kingdom level. large taxa download may slow hit server limits; use narrower queries, BOLDconnectR package full v5 (BCDM) data model. Please cite: Ratnasingham S & Hebert PDN (2007) BOLD: Barcode Life Data System. Molecular Ecology Notes 7:355-364. doi:10.1111/j.1471-8286.2007.01678.x

what: Download the KSGP or GTDB+ reference database — download_ksgp_db
title: Download the KSGP or GTDB+ reference database — download_ksgp_db
text: Downloads KSGP (Karst, Silva, GTDB, PR2) reference database SSU rRNA taxonomic assignment, particularly optimized Archaea communities. KSGP combines near full-length rRNA sequences Karst et al. 2018, re-annotated SILVA prokaryote SSU sequences, cleaned GTDB 16S sequences, PR2 eukaryote 18S sequences, MIDORI2 mitochondrial sequences. Taxonomy based GTDB, providing phylogenetically consistent classification. Also provides access GTDB+ GTDB_cleaned databases built intermediate steps KSGP construction. Three annotation variants available database = "KSGP" file_type = "tax": "sintax" (default): SINTAX-based taxonomic assignments (KSGP Sintax paper). Available versions. "lca": Conservative lowest common ancestor assignments (KSGP LCA). available version "3.1". "ksgp_plus": Similarity-clustered putative taxa (KSGP+). available version "3.1".

title: Download the KSGP or GTDB+ reference database — download_ksgp_db
code: download_ksgp_db(   dest_dir = ".",   database = c("KSGP", "GTDB_plus", "GTDB_cleaned"),   file_type = c("fasta", "tax", "archive"),   annotation = c("sintax", "lca", "ksgp_plus"),   tax_format = c("dada2", "sintax", "none"),   version = "3.1",   verbose = TRUE )

title: Download the KSGP or GTDB+ reference database — download_ksgp_db
text: dest_dir (Character, default ".") Directory save downloaded file. database (Character, default "KSGP") One : "KSGP": Full KSGP SSU database (Archaea + Bacteria + Eukaryota). "GTDB_plus": Cleaned GTDB 16S sequences PR2 MIDORI2. "GTDB_cleaned": Cleaned GTDB 16S sequences (eukaryote supplement). file_type (Character, default "fasta") One : "fasta": FASTA file SSU sequences. "tax": Taxonomy file (.tax) taxonomic annotations. "archive": Complete .tar.gz archive (KSGP files, annotation variants). available database = "KSGP". annotation (Character, default "sintax") Taxonomic annotation method. One "sintax", "lca", "ksgp_plus". applies database = "KSGP" file_type = "tax". "sintax" available version "1.0". tax_format (Character, default "dada2") file_type = "fasta", also download companion .tax file merge taxonomy FASTA headers (matched sequence ID), file feeds MiscMetabar::add_new_taxonomy_pq(). One "dada2", "sintax", "none" (keep accession-headers). Sequences whose ID absent .tax (e.g. SILVA-derived portion) keep accession-headers. Ignored file_type = "tax" / "archive". version (Character, default "3.1") KSGP version. Known versions: "3.1" (2025, recommended) "1.0". verbose (Logical, default TRUE) Print progress messages.

what: Download the LTPlus reference database — download_ltplus_db
title: Download the LTPlus reference database — download_ltplus_db
text: Downloads LTPlus 16S rRNA gene reference FASTA Bacteria Archaea. LTPlus extends -Species Living Tree Project (LTP) type-strain collection best-quality non-type sequences selected SILVA non-redundant GTDB databases, plus highest-quality 16S sequences deposited NCBI 2019 2025. Sequences clustered non-redundantly 98.7% identity threshold, yielding compact database covers known prokaryotic genealogical diversity.

title: Download the LTPlus reference database — download_ltplus_db
text: dest_dir (Character, default ".") Directory save downloaded FASTA file. url (Character) Direct download URL LTPlus FASTA. Defaults February 2026 release served Marine Microbiology Group (IMEDEA, UIB-CSIC). Pass different release URL download another version (see Details). tax_format (Character, default "dada2") Taxonomy format write FASTA headers, file can feed MiscMetabar::add_new_taxonomy_pq(). One : "dada2": unprefixed, semicolon-delimited ranks (>Bacteria;Pseudomonadota;...;). "sintax": >ID;tax=d:Bacteria,p:Pseudomonadota,.... "none": keep original accession-headers. Taxonomy read companion CSV (see csv_url). csv_url (Character) URL LTPlus metadata CSV maps sequence accession full taxonomy. Defaults CSV February 2026 release. used tax_format != "none". to_dna (Logical, default TRUE) Convert downloaded RNA FASTA DNA (transcribe U T) rewrite standard FASTA. Set FALSE keep original RNA file unchanged. Requires Biostrings package. verbose (Logical, default TRUE) Print progress messages.

title: Download the LTPlus reference database — download_ltplus_db
text: file LTPlus 16S FASTA exported underlying curated alignment gap columns removed ("compressed"), sequences unaligned vary length (~140 MB total). served directly (registration) LTP release backend; file name taken server's Content-Disposition header available (e.g. ltplus_10_02_2026_compressed.fasta). released sequences RNA alphabet (U rather T) sequence lines contain whitespace. to_dna = TRUE (default) function transcribes U T rewrites clean, whitespace-free DNA FASTA place, ready DNA-based classifiers dada2 VSEARCH. to_dna = FALSE original RNA file kept -. released FASTA headers carry accession (e.g. >LAJZ3046); taxonomy lives companion CSV. tax_format = "dada2" (default) "sintax" function downloads CSV, maps accession full LTPlus lineage, rewrites headers taxonomy file ready MiscMetabar::add_new_taxonomy_pq(). Use tax_format = "none" keep accession-headers. default url points current release file. list available releases files, see Downloads section https://bioinfo.uib.es/ltp/ query https://biocom.uib.es/opucheck-backend/api/releases; file id appended .../api/releases/ form download URL. ARB database, CSV Newick tree files also available . Please cite: Rosselló-Móra R et al. (2026) pipeline improved 16S rRNA gene-based phylogeny diversity analyses Bacteria Archaea. Research Square. doi:10.21203/rs.3.rs-9370187/v1

title: Download a PR2 reference database — download_pr2_db
text: PR2 uses 9 taxonomic ranks. "dada2" file keeps nine positional, semicolon-delimited lineage (rank prefixes); pass dada2::assignTaxonomy() (via MiscMetabar::add_new_taxonomy_pq() method = "dada2") taxLevels: c("Domain", "Supergroup", "Division", "Subdivision", "Class", "Order", "Family", "Genus", "Species") "UTAX"/"sintax" file targets VSEARCH/USEARCH SINTAX uses 8 standard single-letter rank prefixes (k, d, p, c, o, f, g, s). fit PR2's nine ranks onto , PR2 merges Division Subdivision p: rank (joined -). 9 → 8 mapping : Mind per-method argument calling MiscMetabar::add_new_taxonomy_pq(): method = "dada2" ("dada2" download) keeps 9 ranks — pass 9 names taxLevels (forwarded dada2::assignTaxonomy()). method = "sintax" ("sintax"/"UTAX" download) 8 ranks — pass 8 names taxa_ranks, e.g. c("Domain", "Supergroup", "Division_Subdivision", "Class", "Order", "Family", "Genus", "Species"). dada2 taxLevels argument ignored SINTAX path, default 7 ranks used parsing 8-rank output fails. Please cite: Guillou L et al. (2013) Protist Ribosomal Reference database (PR2). Nucleic Acids Research 41:D1108-D1113. doi:10.1093/nar/gks1160

what: Convert a FASTA database to a specified taxonomy format — format_fasta_db
title: Convert a FASTA database to a specified taxonomy format — format_fasta_db
text: Detects (uses) input taxonomy format rewrites sequence headers requested output format. primary conversion function; format2sintax(), format2dada2(), format2dada2_species() convenience wrappers around . Supported input formats (prefix-based, detectable rank labels): "sintax", "unite", "greengenes2". Supported output formats: "sintax" — VSEARCH/USEARCH SINTAX (>ID;tax=k:Kingdom,p:Phylum,...) "unite" — UNITE default (>ID;k__Kingdom;p__Phylum;...) "greengenes2" — Greengenes2 (>ID d__Domain;p__Phylum;...) "dada2" — Unprefixed semicolon-delimited (>Kingdom;Phylum;...;) "dada2_species" — dada2::addSpecies() (>ID Genus Species) Positional formats ("pr2", "dada2") can detected detect_tax_format() used input conversion lack rank labels.

title: Convert a FASTA database to a specified taxonomy format — format_fasta_db
code: format_fasta_db(   fasta_db = NULL,   taxnames = NULL,   output_format = c("sintax", "unite", "greengenes2", "dada2", "dada2_species"),   input_format = "auto",   output_path = NULL,   id_prefix = "seq" )

title: Convert a FASTA database to a specified taxonomy format — format_fasta_db
text: fasta_db (Character) Path FASTA file (plain gzipped). Mutually exclusive taxnames. taxnames (Character vector) Taxonomy header strings (without leading >). Mutually exclusive fasta_db. output_format (Character) Target format. One "sintax", "unite", "greengenes2", "dada2", "dada2_species". input_format (Character, default "auto") Input format. One "auto" (auto-detect via detect_tax_format()), "sintax", "unite", "greengenes2", "dada2". positional "dada2" input (taxonomy-headers, sequence ID) assigned ranks position (d,p,c,o,f,g,s); see id_prefix generated labels. output_path (Character) provided fasta_db used, write reformatted FASTA path return DNAStringSet invisibly. id_prefix (Character, default "seq") Prefix used build synthetic sequential sequence IDs (e.g. "seq000001") input formats carry per-sequence identifier ("dada2"). Ignored input already provides IDs.

title: Convert a FASTA database to a specified taxonomy format — format_fasta_db

text: file (Character, required) Path FASTA file (plain gzip). rank_prefix (Character, default "k__") prefix identifying taxonomic rank extract (e.g., "k__" kingdom, "p__" phylum). what: Get rank information for a taxonomy format — tax_prefixes
title: Get rank information for a taxonomy format — tax_prefixes
text: Returns taxonomic rank information common reference database formats. prefix-based formats (unite, sintax, greengenes2), returns named character vector prefixes. positional formats (pr2), returns named integer vector rank positions. Use result list_ranks_db() summarize_db() via tax_format parameter. Note: dada2::assignTaxonomy() classifier, taxonomy format. accepts semicolon-separated taxonomy number levels, regardless whether prefixes present . Use taxLevels argument dada2::assignTaxonomy() specify rank names.

title: Get rank information for a taxonomy format — tax_prefixes
code: tax_prefixes(tax_format = c("unite", "sintax", "greengenes2", "pr2"))

title: Get rank information for a taxonomy format — tax_prefixes
text: tax_format (Character) One : "unite": k__/p__/... format used UNITE general FASTA releases. "sintax": d:/k:/p:/... format used VSEARCH SINTAX USEARCH UTAX databases (UNITE SINTAX, PR2 UTAX). Note UNITE SINTAX files use k: (kingdom) first rank include d: (domain). calling summarize_db() UNITE SINTAX file, d: row show 0 sequences — expected. "greengenes2": d__/p__/... format used Greengenes2 (starts domain d__ instead kingdom k__). "pr2": positional format 9 levels specific protist taxonomy: Domain, Supergroup, Division, Subdivision, Class, Order, Family, Genus, Species.

title: Get rank information for a taxonomy format — tax_prefixes
text: prefix-based formats: named character vector rank prefixes. positional formats: named integer vector rank positions.

title: Get rank information for a taxonomy format — tax_prefixes
code: tax_prefixes("unite") #>     k     p     c     o     f     g     s  #> "k__" "p__" "c__" "o__" "f__" "g__" "s__"  tax_prefixes("sintax") #>    d    k    p    c    o    f    g    s  #> "d:" "k:" "p:" "c:" "o:" "f:" "g:" "s:"  tax_prefixes("greengenes2") #>     d     p     c     o     f     g     s  #> "d__" "p__" "c__" "o__" "f__" "g__" "s__"  tax_prefixes("pr2") #>      Domain  Supergroup    Division Subdivision       Class       Order  #>           1           2           3           4           5           6  #>      Family       Genus     Species  #>           7           8           9

title: dbpq (development version)
text: Download functions now produce FASTA files taxonomy headers, ready MiscMetabar::add_new_taxonomy_pq(), via tax_format argument ("dada2"/"sintax"). download_greengenes2_db() strips d__/p__ prefixes plain dada2 (prefixed form rejected assignTaxonomy()); download_ksgp_db() merges companion .tax headers sequence ID; download_ltplus_db() merges taxonomy CSV; download_marjaam_db() uses QIIME release (FASTA + taxonomy table); download_bold_db() pulls full ranked taxonomy BOLD’s combined endpoint. download_marjaam_db() now downloads current MaarjAM QIIME release (dataset argument selects "SSU" (default), "SSU_TYPE", "LSU", "full_ITS" "onlyITS"); previous default URL stopped working. download_bold_db() queries still-available v3.boldsystems.org API HTTPS , default, returns sequences requested marker full ranked-taxonomy headers (BOLD’s main site migrated v5; combined endpoint remains accessible). New article reference databases relate documents sequence-derivation (nestedness) taxonomic-coverage relationships supported databases interactive network two adjacency/coverage matrices, every derivation edge sourced describing paper. Corrects README diagram: EUKARYOME incorporates SILVA, PR2 UNITE (independent), Greengenes2 derives GTDB r207 directly (sharing Karst 2018 source KSGP). New function download_ltplus_db() downloads LTPlus 16S rRNA reference FASTA Bacteria Archaea, extends -Species Living Tree Project (LTP) best-quality non-type sequences SILVA, GTDB NCBI (98.7% identity clustering). (unaligned) FASTA fetched directly LTP release server , default (to_dna = TRUE), transcribed RNA clean DNA FASTA. default (tax_format = "dada2") also merges companion taxonomy CSV headers (100% sequences annotated), file feeds dada2/VSEARCH add_new_taxonomy_pq() directly; url parameter selects releases (Rosselló-Móra et al. 2026, ). New function download_ksgp_db() downloads KSGP (Karst, Silva, GTDB, PR2) SSU reference database GTDB+ GTDB_cleaned components. Supports three annotation variants ("sintax", "lca", "ksgp_plus") complete archive download. KSGP optimized Archaea taxonomic assignment, improving Class Order assignments 2.7x 4.2x SILVA (Grant et al. 2025, ). download_silva_db() gains format = "sintax", downloads official arb-silva DADA2 toSpecies trainset converts locally VSEARCH/USEARCH SINTAX database (7 ranks d,p,c,o,f,g,s, written *_sintax.fasta.gz). Synthetic sequence labels (SILVA<version>_<target>_NNNNNN) generated dada2 trainset carries accession. download_silva_db() now sources dada2-formatted files official arb-silva DADA2 release instead Zenodo, supports target = "LSU" dada2, dada2_species, sintax formats (previously SSU ). format_fasta_db() format2sintax() accept input_format = "dada2" (positional, prefix-less taxonomy) gain id_prefix argument label records carry sequence ID. detect_tax_format() now recognises positional dada2 headers (returns "dada2") instead falling "unknown". New function add_sh_to_taxonomy() (marked experimental) annotates query sequences UNITE Species Hypothesis (SH) names running vsearch --usearch_global UNITE reference database extracting SH identifiers matched sequence headers. Detects ambiguous assignments multiple top hits disagree SH name. Ports logic nf-core/ampliseq add_sh_to_taxonomy.py script R. count_pattern_db() count_seq_db() longer emit spurious warning pattern zero matches (example counting sequences empty file). count_pattern_db() filter_db() now quote file paths search patterns passed shell commands, paths patterns containing spaces shell metacharacters handled correctly. New function find_vsearch() locates vsearch executable system PATH verifies user-supplied path. New function is_vsearch_installed() checks whether vsearch available system. list_ranks_db() now emits informative message taxa match requested rank prefix, suggesting detect_tax_format() identify file’s taxonomy format. summarize_db() now handles empty FASTA databases gracefully, reporting zero sequences instead emitting warnings Inf length statistics.

title: dbpq 0.1
text: Initial development version. cutadapt_rm_primers_db() now checks exit code cutadapt stops informative error binary missing fails, instead silently continuing. download_unite_db() now emits message type = "static" taxon_group = "fungi" clarify UNITE v10.0 ship separate static/dynamic archives fungi. filter_db() documentation now runnable example using bundled example_unite.fasta file. get_file_extension() longer emits spurious warning double-extension files (e.g. .fasta.gz), standard format compressed databases. example FASTA file (inst/extdata/example_unite.fasta, 5 sequences UNITE format) now bundled package, enabling runnable examples count_seq_db(), count_pattern_db(), detect_tax_format(), filter_db(), list_ranks_db(), summarize_db(). tax_prefixes("sintax") documentation now clarifies UNITE SINTAX files use k: (kingdom) first rank include d: (domain) level; d: 0 sequences row summarize_db() output therefore expected. detect_tax_format() auto-detects taxonomy format ("default", "sintax", "greengenes2") FASTA headers. download_diatbarcode_db() downloads Diat.barcode rbcL reference database diatoms. download_eukaryome_db() now lists SINTAX format download page (https://eukaryome.org/sintax/) instructions. download_greengenes2_db() downloads Greengenes2 16S rRNA database (dada2 format Zenodo plain FASTA FTP). download_midori2_db() downloads MIDORI2 mitochondrial reference database COI markers. download_pr2_db() now accepts format = "sintax" alias "UTAX". Documentation now mentions pr2database R package complementary tool. download_rdp_db() downloads RDP 16S rRNA database (dada2-formatted training sets Zenodo). download_unite_db() gains taxonomic_format parameter ("default" "sintax") download SINTAX-formatted FASTA files directly, ready use vsearch --sintax. list_ranks_db() summarize_db() gain tax_format parameter handle different taxonomy header formats ("unite", "sintax", "greengenes2", "pr2", "auto"). list_ranks_db() also gains rank_position parameter positional (prefix-less) taxonomy headers. tax_prefixes() returns rank prefixes given taxonomy format, use list_ranks_db() summarize_db(). count_unwanted_tax() scans taxonomy table (phyloseq object FASTA reference database) common problematic values "unclassified", "unknown", "Incertae_sedis", NA-like strings, empty QIIME-style rank prefixes. Returns tibble summarising matches per pattern rank. Suggests MiscMetabar::verify_tax_table() cleaning input phyloseq object. Default patterns sourced MiscMetabar::unwanted_tax_patterns MiscMetabar installed, built-fallback otherwise. count_pattern_db() counts sequences matching pattern FASTA files. count_seq_db() counts total sequences FASTA file. filter_db() filters FASTA database taxonomic pattern. format_fasta_db() new unified function convert FASTA taxonomy headers supported format ("sintax", "unite", "greengenes2", "dada2", "dada2_species"), auto-detection input format. format2dada2(), format2dada2_species(), format2sintax() now accept input_format argument ("auto", "sintax", "unite", "greengenes2") instead from_sintax, support Greengenes2 additional input format. now wrappers around format_fasta_db(). cutadapt_rm_primers_db() removes primer sequences FASTA database using cutadapt.
